_meta.description: Single source of truth for the IES Final Project MVP. Every number displayed in the app reads from this file.

xendee_run.report_title: IES_Final Project - PV with Canopy with Diesel

data_provenance.real_public: Silicon Valley Power tariffs (CB-3), CAISO LMPs, NOAA weather, NREL solar resource
data_provenance.facility_public: 48 MW peak load envelope and Santa Clara siting are public — Digital Realty SJC37 and similar Walsh Ave facilities reported in Bloomberg, Fortune, Tom's Hardware (Nov 2025)
data_provenance.representative_assumptions: Internal load shape, GPU cluster split, PUE, cooling zones constructed from Uptime Institute and NVIDIA published benchmarks; no operational data from any specific facility is used
data_provenance.xendee_outputs: Portfolio sizing, CapEx, NPV, LCOE, payback, IRR, annual energy/demand charges, fuel costs, generator runtime, CO2 emissions, ride-through resilience

_meta.field_source_legend: Each major block tags individual fields with _source: 'xendee' (from the Apr 27 2026 Xendee run) or _source: 'assumption' (constructed from public benchmarks).

site.name: Santa Clara Hyperscale Facility (Representative Model)
site.facility_reference: Modeled on a 48 MW Santa Clara data center (e.g., Digital Realty SJC37, Stack Infrastructure SVY10A); load shape and internal architecture are constructed from public benchmarks
site.tagline: Behind-the-meter DER deployment to bypass the SVP interconnection queue

_source_notes.peak_load_mw: xendee (48 MW input, confirmed in report cover)
_source_notes.interconnection_queue_years: real_public (SVP grid queue per public reporting)

grid_operator.name: CAISO
grid_operator.note: Dynamic pricing, strong duck curve, frequent negative midday prices in spring

utility.name: Silicon Valley Power
utility.current_constraints: Capacity-tight market; ~$450M in grid upgrades not delivering until 2028; ~3.5-year interconnection queue for new large loads
utility._source: xendee inputs (energy price, demand charge); real_public (queue timeline, upgrade budget)

solar_resource.note: Strong solar resource, but on-site footprint is the binding constraint

site_footprint.note: Both rooftop and parking-canopy footprints are at 100% utilization in the optimized portfolio. Additional on-site PV is physically infeasible without offsite PPA.

market_context.comparable_btm_deployment: ECL announced a 35 MW BTM data center in Santa Clara built in under a year by skipping the SVP queue (Nov 2025).
market_context.grid_pilot: SVP is running a flexible-data-center pilot with Emerald AI, signalling utility appetite for DER-enabled sites.
market_context._source: real_public (Tom's Hardware Nov 2025 and SVP press releases)

gpu_clusters.note: Large language model training pod, NVIDIA H100

proposed_portfolio._source: xendee (Apr 27 2026 run, PV with Canopy with Diesel scenario)
proposed_portfolio._summary: 5.35 MW PV (rooftop + canopy) + 12.6 MWh / 3.16 MW BESS + 60 MW diesel backup. Sized to ride-through a 3-day grid outage and to fully utilize on-site solar real estate.

diesel.role: Black-start and 3-day grid outage ride-through; not used for routine peaking
diesel.note: Diesel was selected over fuel cell (earlier scope) because fuel-cell costs were too speculative for a 2026 financing decision.

alternative_portfolios.name: Diesel-Only (fastest-deploy)
alternative_portfolios.note: Indicative comparison only — not Xendee-optimized. Lowest CapEx and fastest deployment, but no renewable component and full SVP energy bill.

alternative_portfolios.name: PV + BESS Only (no diesel)
alternative_portfolios.note: Indicative comparison only — not Xendee-optimized. Cleanest portfolio but cannot meet the 3-day ride-through requirement; would also require 4x larger BESS at ~$20M premium.

economics._thesis_note: Energy-arbitrage NPV is intentionally negative because Silicon Valley Power is one of California's cheapest utilities (~$0.17/kWh blended). The investment case rests on time-to-power and resilience, not on energy savings.
economics._npv_explainer: NPV at year 20 of -$19.68M reflects the cost premium of the optimized microgrid vs. continuing to buy 100% from SVP at current tariffs. The negative NPV is the price of resilience (3-day ride-through) plus the entry ticket to the time-to-power capture below.

annual_cost_comparison_thousands._note: Optimized total includes annualized capital costs. OPEX alone falls by ~$1.4M/yr.

annual_savings_breakdown._note: Net OPEX savings of ~$1.4M/yr. Negative entries are new costs (diesel fuel, DER maintenance) that did not exist in the reference (utility-only) case.

co2_balance._note: Net emissions barely change because the 60 MW diesel backup, although running only 72 hr/yr, emits ~2,500 tCO2/yr — almost exactly offsetting the PV contribution. The small (~0.1%) increase is within Xendee modeling tolerance.

cfe_metrics.onsite_renewable_share_note: On-site PV (8.94 GWh/yr) divided by total load (360.5 GWh/yr). Caps out at this level because both rooftop and canopy footprints are at 100% utilization.
cfe_metrics.cfe_with_svp_grid_mix_note: Includes SVP's roughly 30% renewable RPS content plus the 2.48% on-site contribution. Indicative only — full hourly 24/7 matching was not modeled.
cfe_metrics.industry_24_7_benchmark_note: Hyperscaler 24/7 hourly CFE matching ranges from ~20% (typical) to ~64% (Google 2024 disclosure). On-site DER alone cannot meet hyperscaler CFE goals at this site footprint; PPAs and grid-mix evolution carry the rest.

resilience.industry_outage_cost_note: Typical hyperscaler outage cost runs $200k-$1M+ per hour (Uptime Institute). The $21.9k/hr resilience premium is dwarfed by avoided outage cost on any single failure event.

time_to_power_thesis._source: assumption (constructed from public reporting on hyperscaler revenue per MW)
time_to_power_thesis._role: Primary value pillar — this is why the project pencils despite negative energy NPV.
time_to_power_thesis._note: Coupled to years_saved=2 and thesis_value_per_dollar=2. If years_saved changes, update both early_revenue_captured_millions and thesis_value_per_dollar to maintain consistency.
time_to_power_thesis.headline: $17.3M net value: $37M early-revenue capture minus $19.7M Xendee energy NPV.
time_to_power_thesis._assumptions: Lost-revenue figure assumes ~$18.5M/yr of contracted hyperscaler revenue at risk while waiting for grid interconnection. Constructed from public lease/colocation rate benchmarks (~$1.5-2M per MW-year recurring) applied to a partial fill of a 48 MW facility during ramp.

hero_callouts.secondary: $37M revenue captured by skipping the queue

sensitivity_analysis._note: Pre-computed lookup table for Scenario Studio. Each delta represents NPV change ($M) for a 1-unit move in the variable. Coefficients are illustrative — derived from simple sensitivities, not from re-running Xendee.

svp_energy_price._note: Higher SVP rates make the BTM investment more attractive (less negative NPV).

early_revenue._note: Each $1M/yr of early hyperscaler revenue adds ~$2M to the time-to-power thesis (2 years saved).

live_operations._source: assumption (illustrative real-time operational snapshot; not from a live SCADA feed)

recommendations.title: Shift GPU-Cluster-01 Training Job
recommendations.description: Move large training workload to 2:00-6:00 AM when CAISO prices and grid carbon are lowest.

recommendations.title: Pre-cool Hall A
recommendations.description: Lower setpoint by 1 degC now while solar generation peaks. Reduce cooling during evening peak hours.

recommendations.title: Discharge Battery at 5 PM
recommendations.description: CAISO peak pricing window starts at 5 PM. Schedule BESS discharge at 3 MW for 4 hours to shave evening demand peak.

recommendations.title: Defer Cluster-03 Maintenance
recommendations.description: Delay scheduled maintenance to Saturday off-peak window. Maintains training capacity during weekday peak.

recommendations.title: Enable Free Cooling Mode
recommendations.description: Outdoor conditions suitable for economizer mode starting 8 PM. Reduce mechanical cooling load by 30%.

dispatch_24h._note: Representative 24-hour dispatch profile reflecting Xendee-sized portfolio (5.35 MW PV, 3.16 MW BESS, 60 MW diesel backup). Diesel sits at 0 MW on a normal operating day; it only runs during grid outages (72 hr/yr).
dispatch_24h._source: assumption (illustrative; the Xendee dispatch graph in the Apr 27 2026 PDF rendered without data)

scenarios._note: Scenario Studio results are computed from input-driven multipliers, not re-runs of Xendee. Daily $ values are derived from the annual reference cost ($63.2M reference, $61.8M optimized OPEX) divided by 365.
scenarios._source: assumption (illustrative coefficients on top of Xendee annual totals)

insights_30day._note: Representative monthly snapshot during normal operation (no grid outage events). Numbers derived from Xendee annual totals divided by 12.
insights_30day._source: assumption (illustrative monthly run-rate from xendee annuals)

pathway_comparison._source: Xendee runs + external PPA benchmarks (diesel_primary: Xendee Apr 27 2026; gas_turbine: Jose's Xendee Apr 29 2026; fuel_cells: external Bloom Energy PPA benchmarks, illustrative)
pathway_comparison._note: CO2 values are kt net vs. grid reference over 20 years — negative means net carbon reduction relative to grid baseline. Diesel and gas turbine figures are from Xendee. Fuel cell CO2 is illustrative from public benchmarks, not a Xendee run. Deployment months are midpoints of estimated ranges (gas turbine: 9–15 mo; fuel cells: 12–18 mo).

pathways.name: Fuel Cells (Bloom SOFC)
pathways.tagline: Zero CapEx PPA — no on-site footprint
pathways._source: external benchmarks (Bloom Energy PPA model; illustrative, not Xendee-modeled)

pathways.name: Gas Turbine (45 MW)
pathways.tagline: On-site 45 MW gas plant with PV layer — Xendee-modeled
